title: This Unstoppable Vanguard ETF Is Obliterating the S&P 500 in 2026, but a Looming Change in Interest Rates Could Halt Its Momentum
author: Anthony Di Pizio
description: The Vanguard Russell 2000 ETF is outperforming the S&P 500 with an 18% year-to-date gain, driven by domestic-focused small-cap companies benefiting from U.S. government policies and AI infrastructure demand. However, rising inflation and anticipated interest rate hikes pose a significant risk, as 32% of Russell 2000 companies carry floating-rate debt that will become more expensive, potentially hampering their growth and earnings.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong performance year-to-date (18% gain) and diversification benefits are offset by significant headwinds from anticipated interest rate hikes that disproportionately affect small-cap companies with floating-rate debt.
Stock has surged 1,400% over the last 12 months due to strong demand for clean energy solutions from AI data center operators seeking alternative power sources.

title: Why Shares in This Under-the-Radar Data Center Stock Power Surged 16% Today
author: Lee Samaha
description: Advanced Energy Industries (AEIS) surged 16% after beating Q4 earnings expectations and reporting strong growth in data center and semiconductor markets. The company, which supplies precision power equipment for data centers and semiconductor production, saw data center revenue grow 101% year-over-year. Management expects data center revenue to grow over 30% in 2026, driven by AI-led demand and upcoming 800V HDC data center technology launching in 2027.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company beat Q4 earnings expectations, expanded margins despite China factory closure, reported 101% YoY data center revenue growth, and management provided bullish 2026 guidance with expected 30%+ data center revenue growth. Strong positioning in high-demand AI and semiconductor markets with next-generation technology in development.

title: TTM Technologies, Dutch Bros, Advanced Energy Industries, and American Healthcare REIT Set to Join S&P MidCap 400; Others to Join S&P SmallCap 600
author: Prnewswire
description: S&P Dow Jones Indices announced index changes effective January 30 and February 2, 2026. TTM Technologies, Dutch Bros, Advanced Energy Industries, and American Healthcare REIT will be promoted to the S&P MidCap 400, while Amneal Pharmaceuticals, Apellis Pharmaceuticals, and LegalZoom.com will join the S&P SmallCap 600. These changes follow several pending M&A transactions and corporate actions.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Promotion from S&P SmallCap 600 to S&P MidCap 400 indicates improved market capitalization and investor appeal, typically attracting more institutional investment.
Promotion to S&P MidCap 400 signals growth and increased market recognition, likely to attract index-tracking funds and institutional investors.
Promotion from S&P SmallCap 600 to S&P MidCap 400 reflects company growth and improved market position.
Promotion to S&P MidCap 400 indicates improved market capitalization and increased investor interest in the REIT.
Addition to S&P SmallCap 600 is a standard index inclusion with no clear positive or negative implications.

title: Forget Nvidia And Broadcom, These 5 Small-Cap Chip Stocks Could Rally Big This Year
author: Piero Cingari
description: Bank of America semiconductor analyst Vivek Arya identifies five small- and mid-cap chip stocks positioned for outperformance in 2026, driven by AI infrastructure spending, rising chip complexity, and aerospace/defense demand. The stocks include Credo Technology (AI connectivity leader), MKS Inc (wafer equipment), Advanced Energy Industries (AI power demand), Teradyne (chip testing), and MACOM (optics and defense exposure).

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Fastest-growing name in the group with 75% market share in active electrical cables for AI servers. Market expected to expand to $7B by 2030 from $1.2B currently. Bank of America sees 44% upside to $200 price target with $10+ earnings power potential.

Positioned to benefit from multiyear wafer fabrication equipment upcycle and advanced packaging demand. Raised Q4 guidance and EBITDA expectations. Bank of America increased price target from $215 to $255 with 8% revenue and 15% EPS growth forecast through 2027.

Offers exposure to AI-driven data center power demand and semiconductor equipment spending rebound. Bank of America raised price target from $260 to $300 with 11% revenue and 19% EPS growth forecast through 2027. Management guidance for 25-30% data center growth in 2026.

Well-positioned as testing requirements for AI chips outpace shipment growth. Bank of America expects 20% revenue growth and 39% EPS growth through 2027 with 12% upside to $260 price target. Additional upside from robotics and Amazon warehouse automation.

Offers dual exposure to data center optics and aerospace/defense tailwinds. Data center segment grew 48% in 2025. Bank of America forecasts 15% revenue and 22% EPS growth through 2027 with 15% upside to $260 price target. Defense contracts and Starlink expansion provide additional growth drivers.

Mentioned as an established large-cap chip giant with already high valuations. Article suggests investors seeking incremental upside may find better opportunities in smaller-cap alternatives rather than continuing to chase NVIDIA.

Referenced as a high-flying chip giant with limited incremental upside potential. Article positions it as an alternative to consider versus smaller-cap semiconductor names with higher growth potential.
Mentioned alongside NVIDIA and Broadcom as an established large-cap chip stock where investors may have already captured significant gains and should consider smaller alternatives.

title: FormFactor Announces Participation in 14th Annual NYC Summit
author: Formfactor, Inc.
description: FormFactor will join 14 other technology companies at the 14th Annual NYC Summit investor conference on December 16th, 2025, featuring small group meetings and networking opportunities for investors and analysts.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company is actively participating in a prominent investor conference, showcasing engagement with investors and industry peers
